svg.title: Fashion-MNIST confusion matrix from a 2-hidden-layer MLP
svg.desc: A 10x10 confusion matrix on the Fashion-MNIST test set. Rows are true classes, columns are predicted classes. The diagonal cells (correct predictions) are shaded green with the count printed inside; off-diagonal cells (errors) are shaded tan with opacity scaled by count. The largest confusions are among the four shirt-like classes (T-shirt/top, Pullover, Coat, Shirt) which a small MLP cannot reliably distinguish. The right panel shows per-class accuracy as horizontal bars sorted by class index, with Shirt as the hardest class at 71 percent and Bag as the easiest at 97.8 percent. A small list at the bottom-right shows the top five most-confused class pairs.
svg.text: Same architecture as project 01; the harder dataset exposes the shirt-vs-pullover-vs-coat-vs-T-shirt blur the model cannot resolve.
rows = true class, columns = predicted class
sorted by class index; Shirt is the hard one
